TITLE: docs: Bring README, ADRs, and intro docs current with the fork
LABELS: roadmap, type:docs, size:M, phase:A-foundation
---
## Context

This repo is a fork of https://github.com/bnsreenu/digitalsreeni-image-annotator that has diverged massively (PyQt6 migration, in-process inference, Grounding-DINO text-prompt detection, SAM 2 fine-tuning with MLflow tracking, undo/redo, annotations table with Detail %, keypoint/pose annotation, canvas selection + shape editing, 94-test suite). The user-facing docs have not kept up: `README.md` is still essentially upstream's file — it omits every fork feature, links to the upstream repo as "Project Link", tells users to `pip install` the upstream PyPI package, and still warns that Linux "may not work correctly". Several ADRs and intro docs contradict the current codebase. A newcomer reading the repo front door gets a description of a different, older application.

## Current state

Every claim below verified against the working tree.

**`README.md`** (275 lines) — stale upstream content:
- Line 5: PyPI version badge; lines 77–81 instruct `pip install digitalsreeni-image-annotator` — that installs **upstream's** PyPI release, not this fork.
- Line 17: "Watch the demo (of v0.8.0)" — two minor versions behind.
- Features list (lines 24–66): no mention of Grounding-DINO text-prompted detection, SAM fine-tuning, MLflow tracking, undo/redo, annotations table + Detail % simplification, keypoints/pose, canvas selection / handle-based shape editing, dark-mode soft stylesheet, image list filter/sort.
- Lines 44 and 150: "YOLO (beta) training" — no longer beta; train/val split and custom models exist.
- Keyboard-shortcut list (lines 186–198): missing Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y undo/redo, Ctrl+Shift+= / - / 0 font sizing, Delete (instant, undoable), Shift+Click / rubber-band canvas selection, keypoint-tool shortcuts, and the current Esc semantics (cancel **and** return to selection mode). Source of truth: the `CLAUDE.md` "Keyboard Shortcuts" tables.
- Line 202: "The application may not work correctly on Linux systems. Extensive testing has not been done yet." — false; CI runs the suite on ubuntu-latest and line 69 already documents the Qt6 Linux runtime deps.
- Lines 213, 258, 264–273: clone URL, "Project Link", and citation all point at `bnsreenu/...` — no fork attribution section distinguishing this repo from upstream.

**`docs/09_architecture_decisions.md`**:
- ADR-004 "No Automated Testing Framework" (lines 58–79): Status "Accepted (Technical Debt)", decision "Rely on manual testing only" — flatly contradicted by the 94-test pytest + pytest-qt suite and the CI matrix.
- Appendix "Decisions Under Consideration → Consider pytest-qt for Utility Testing" (lines 1413–1428): proposes adding tests that already exist.
- ADR-001 superseding note (line 9) claims "PyQt6 eliminated the WinError 1114 DLL load-order conflict" — contradicted by ADR-017 (lines 647+), which documents that the conflict persists and is fixed by eager torch import instead.
- Line 308 (ADR-014 consequences): "the existing test suite (65 pytest-qt tests...)" — stale count.

**`docs/01_introduction_and_goals.md`**: Key Features (lines 9–27) omit DINO detection, SAM fine-tuning + MLflow, undo/redo, keypoints/pose, annotations table/Detail %, canvas selection & editing. (Line 7 already names the fork correctly — keep.)

**`docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md`**: "Upstream Fork Divergence" section (lines 331–348) ends with the placeholder `- (Document any fork-specific features here)` (line 348).

**`CHANGELOG.md`**: does not exist (verified repo root).

## Proposed design

Rewrite `README.md` as the **fork's** README with this section-by-section outline:

1. **Title + badges** — keep name; drop the PyPI badge (fork is not published); keep Python/license badges; add a line: fork of upstream with link, one sentence on what the fork adds.
2. **What is this** — 2–3 sentence description (PyQt6 annotation app, SAM 2 assisted, text-prompted DINO detection, multi-dim images, keypoints).
3. **Feature highlights** — grouped bullets: *Annotation tools* (polygon, rectangle, paint/eraser, keypoints/pose, merge, vertex + handle editing, undo/redo); *AI-assisted* (SAM 2 box/points, Grounding-DINO text prompts with single/batch + review, SAM fine-tuning with MLflow, YOLO training/prediction); *Images* (TIFF/CZI stacks, dimension dialog, slice navigation, DICOM converter, registration, interpolation); *Data management* (COCO/YOLO/Pascal VOC/labeled/semantic export, COCO/YOLO import, dataset splitter/patcher/augmenter, annotations table with Area + Detail %, project search); *UI* (dark mode, font scaling, filter/sort).
4. **Installation** — from source only: clone **this fork**, `pip install -e .`; Linux Qt6 runtime deps (keep current line 69 content); GPU/CUDA section (keep lines 85–108 — still accurate); remove the PyPI install path.
5. **Quick start** — run commands (`digitalsreeni-image-annotator` / `sreeni` / `python -m ...`), 5-step golden path (new project → add images → add class → SAM-assisted annotate → export).
6. **Keyboard shortcuts** — regenerate both tables from `CLAUDE.md` "Keyboard Shortcuts" verbatim.
7. **Documentation** — link `docs/README.md` (arc42 index), `CLAUDE.md`, `TESTING.md`.
8. **Development** — clone, `pip install -e .` (+ dev extras if the pyproject migration landed — check for `pyproject.toml` and match), run tests (`pytest tests/ -v`), feature-branch workflow.
9. **Fork attribution & upstream** — explicit section: upstream repo link, author credit (Dr. Sreenivas Bhattiprolu, YouTube), what diverged; keep the citation block for upstream's original work.
10. **License** — MIT, unchanged.

Plus: surgical ADR/doc edits and a new `CHANGELOG.md` (Keep a Changelog format) with a single `0.9.0` section summarising the fork's state (PyQt6, in-process inference, DINO, SAM fine-tuning + MLflow, undo/redo, keypoints, annotations table, test suite), and an `Unreleased` stub.

## Implementation steps

1. Create branch `feature/docs-fork-currency`.
2. Rewrite `README.md` per the outline above. Keep the demo GIF/screenshot references only if the files exist in `screenshots/` (verify with `ls screenshots/`); label videos as "upstream demo (v0.8.0)" or drop them.
3. Copy the shortcut tables from `CLAUDE.md` into README section 6 — do not invent or reword bindings.
4. `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md`:
   - ADR-004: change Status to `Superseded` with a short superseding note: the project now has a pytest + pytest-qt suite (94 tests, `tests/unit` / `tests/integration` / `tests/ui`, CI on 3 OS × Python 3.10–3.14) and an AST import gate (cross-ref [ADR-016](#adr-016-static-ast-inspection-of-inline-imports-as-quality-gate-for-refactor-prs)). Keep the historical body under an "Original decision (historical)" heading, mirroring ADR-001's style (lines 3–9).
   - Delete the "Consider pytest-qt for Utility Testing" block (lines 1413–1428) from "Decisions Under Consideration"; leave the "Relative Paths with Image Copying" block in place.
   - Fix ADR-001's superseding note (line 9): reword so it does not claim PyQt6 eliminated the DLL conflict; state the migration unblocked ADR-013 and cross-ref ADR-017 for the surviving conflict + eager-torch fix.
   - Line 308: update "65 pytest-qt tests" to the current count (run `pytest --collect-only -q tests/ | tail -1` and use the real number) or reword to "the pytest-qt suite".
5. `docs/01_introduction_and_goals.md`: extend Key Features (lines 9–27) with the missing fork features (DINO text-prompt detection, SAM fine-tuning + MLflow, undo/redo, keypoints/pose with COCO/YOLO-pose export-import, annotations table with Detail %, canvas selection/handle editing). Keep it a compact bullet list, not a README duplicate.
6. `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md`: replace the placeholder at line 348 with a real fork-divergence list. Derive it from evidence, not memory: `git log --oneline --merges master` plus the ADR index (ADR-013/014 in-process inference + PyQt6, ADR-018/019 canvas refactor, ADR-021 SAM fine-tuning, ADR-022–026 selection/editing/undo, ADR-027 MLflow, ADR-029 keypoints). 8–15 bullets, each one line.
7. Create `CHANGELOG.md` at repo root ("Keep a Changelog" header, `## [Unreleased]`, `## [0.9.0]` with grouped Added/Changed entries summarising the divergence — reuse the docs/11 list).
8. Cross-check: `grep -rn "bnsreenu" README.md docs/ CHANGELOG.md` — every remaining hit must be an intentional upstream attribution, not a self-reference.
9. Run smoke tests (docs-only change, but cheap insurance) and render-check the README locally (e.g. on the GitHub branch page) for broken tables/links.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] README describes the fork: DINO detection, SAM fine-tuning, MLflow, undo/redo, Detail %, keypoints/pose all appear in Features.
- [ ] README contains no `pip install digitalsreeni-image-annotator` (PyPI) instruction and no PyPI badge; install is clone + `pip install -e .`.
- [ ] README shortcut tables match `CLAUDE.md`'s tables entry-for-entry.
- [ ] README has a fork-attribution section linking upstream, and its clone URL points at this fork.
- [ ] The "may not work correctly on Linux" sentence is gone; Linux runtime deps remain documented.
- [ ] ADR-004 status is `Superseded` with a cross-reference to the real test suite and ADR-016; the "Consider pytest-qt" appendix block is deleted.
- [ ] ADR-001's superseding note no longer claims PyQt6 eliminated the DLL conflict and cross-refs ADR-017.
- [ ] `docs/01` Key Features includes the fork features listed in step 5.
- [ ] `docs/11` line 348 placeholder is replaced with ≥8 concrete divergence bullets.
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` exists with `Unreleased` + `0.9.0` sections.
- [ ] `grep -rn "no automated tests" docs/ README.md` returns nothing.
- [ ] `pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v` passes.

## Test plan

Docs-only change; verification is grep + render:

```bash
pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v
grep -rn "pip install digitalsreeni-image-annotator" README.md          # expect no hits
grep -rn "may not work correctly on Linux" README.md                    # expect no hits
grep -n "Superseded" docs/09_architecture_decisions.md | grep -i adr-004 || sed -n '55,85p' docs/09_architecture_decisions.md
grep -rn "Document any fork-specific features here" docs/               # expect no hits
grep -rn "Consider pytest-qt" docs/                                     # expect no hits
```

## Docs to update

This issue *is* the docs update. Additionally keep `docs/README.md` (index) consistent if it summarises chapter contents, and do **not** modify `CLAUDE.md` beyond what other issues own — the shortcut tables are copied *from* it.

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- **Cross-document consistency**: when several docs reference the same concept, re-grep for stale references after editing (the senior-reviewer agent checks exactly this). E.g. fixing ADR-004 while leaving "65 pytest-qt tests" at docs/09:308 is a half-done job.
- Do not rewrite ADR history — ADRs are records. Supersede with a note (ADR-001, lines 3–9, is the house style); never delete an accepted ADR's body. The only deletion allowed here is the "Under Consideration" appendix item, which is a proposal, not a decision.
- The fork-divergence list and CHANGELOG must be derived from `git log` and the ADRs — do not invent features or dates. If unsure whether something landed, check the code.
- Keep upstream attribution generous and accurate: original author, upstream link, citation block. This is a fork, not a rebrand.
- README shortcuts: `CLAUDE.md` is authoritative; if you spot a CLAUDE.md/code mismatch while copying, flag it in the PR description instead of silently "fixing" either side.
- If `pyproject.toml` exists on master by the time you branch, match its install instructions (`pip install -e ".[dev]"` for dev); if not, keep `pip install -e .` — do not pre-document unlanded packaging changes.

## Dependencies

None strictly. Soft ordering: if #38 (pyproject migration) merges first, use its install commands in README section 8; coordinate to avoid conflicting README edits.

_Workflow: follow CLAUDE.md — feature branch, run smoke tests (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`), update arc42 docs, run the senior-reviewer agent before the PR._
